title: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtim
stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholders include: end users (ergonomics, suction performance on common home debris, noise, ease of emptying/cleaning, charging convenience), purchasers/households (price/value, durability, warranty), regulators/certifiers (EU compliance), manufacturer/service (assemblability, reliability, maintainability, battery lifecycle), and retailers/logistics (packaging, transport safety for Li-ion). Core requirements already stated: >=20 min runtime, <1.5 kg mass, EU safety standards; plus operating environment: indoor, 10–40°C, up to 80% RH. Likely additional requirement areas to formalize: cleaning performance metrics (suction/airflow/pickup on hard floors vs carpets, crevice tool performance), battery/charging (charge time, replaceability, cycle life, battery safety/transport), usability (one-handed operation, center of mass, handle design, bin volume, filtration/HEPA needs, noise limits), reliability (drop resistance, clogging tolerance, filter life), and compliance scope (applicable directives/standards: Low Voltage Directive if applicable, EMC Directive, RoHS, REACH, WEEE, Battery Regulation, IEC/EN 60335 series for household appliances, IEC 62133/UN 38.3 for batteries, and potentially Ecodesign/energy-related requirements).
clarification_questions: What cleaning performance targets are required (e.g., surfaces: hard floor only vs hard+carpet; typical debris; minimum suction/airflow or pickup efficiency; acceptable noise level)?

What charging and battery expectations apply (charge time, removable vs fixed battery, desired cycle life/retained runtime after N cycles, charging interface such as dock/USB-C, and whether the 20 min runtime is at max power or standard mode)?
What filtration and hygiene requirements are needed (HEPA grade or allergen capture level, bin capacity, washability of filters, and constraints on dust exposure during emptying)?

table.title: Handheld Vacuum Cleaner (Battery-Powered) — System Requirements
rows: The vacuum cleaner SHALL provide a minimum continuous runtime of 20 min on the standard (normal) power setting.
With a fully charged new battery at 23 ± 2 °C, device operates continuously for ≥ 20.0 min on normal mode until automatic cutoff.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL recharge from 0% to 100% state of charge within 4 h using the supplied charger.
From automatic low-voltage cutoff to charge termination, full charge time ≤ 4.0 h at 23 ± 2 °C.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL provide at least two user-selectable suction modes (Normal and Boost).
User can select Normal and Boost via dedicated control; mode selection is repeatable for 100 consecutive toggles without malfunction.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL achieve a sealed suction of at least 10 kPa in Boost mode.
Measured sealed suction at nozzle interface ≥ 10.0 kPa in Boost mode with clean filter and empty bin.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL have an A-weighted sound power level of ≤ 80 dB(A) on Normal mode.
Sound power level LWA ≤ 80 dB(A) per ISO 3744 (or equivalent) at 23 ± 2 °C on Normal mode.
The handheld unit (including installed battery and dust bin) SHALL have a mass less than 1.5 kg.
Measured mass on calibrated scale < 1.50 kg.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL support one-handed operation for typical cleaning tasks.
All primary controls (power and mode) operable with one hand; user can lift, start, and stop device using one hand in ≥ 9/10 attempts across 10 representative users.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL include a removable dust bin with a minimum capacity of 0.30 L.
Measured usable dust-bin volume ≥ 0.30 L when filled to manufacturer indicated max line.
The dust bin SHALL be emptyable without direct hand contact with collected debris.
When emptied per instructions, user does not need to touch debris; verified by task analysis and observation for 10/10 trials.
The product SHALL comply with EN/IEC 60335-1 and EN/IEC 60335-2-2 safety requirements for household vacuum cleaners.
Valid CB report or EU test report demonstrating pass results to EN/IEC 60335-1 and EN/IEC 60335-2-2 with no nonconformities.
The product SHALL meet EU EMC requirements under the EMC Directive (2014/30/EU).
Pass emissions and immunity tests to EN 55014-1 and EN 55014-2 (or applicable harmonized equivalents) with no unresolved deviations.
The product SHALL comply with RoHS (2011/65/EU) substance restrictions.
Technical documentation and supplier declarations show all restricted substances below RoHS maximum concentration values (e.g., Pb < 0.1% w/w in homogeneous materials).
The vacuum cleaner SHALL withstand a drop from 1.0 m onto hardwood without loss of function.
After 3 drops (each on a different face) from 1.0 m onto 30 mm hardwood over concrete, device powers on and provides suction; no exposed live parts or battery damage.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL meet indoor operating environmental limits of 10–40 °C and 20–80% RH (non-condensing).
Device meets functional performance (on/off, suction, and runtime ≥ 20 min on normal) at 10 °C/20% RH and 40 °C/80% RH, non-condensing.
The charger input SHALL support 220–240 VAC, 50 Hz mains power for EU households.
Charger label and test confirm operation over 220–240 VAC, 50 Hz with stable charging and no protective trips.
The lithium-ion battery pack SHALL comply with UN 38.3 transportation testing requirements.
UN 38.3 test summary provided showing pass for T.1–T.8 (as applicable to cell/pack configuration).
The battery SHALL retain at least 80% of initial capacity after 300 full charge-discharge cycles.
After 300 cycles (0–100% equivalent at 23 ± 2 °C), measured discharge capacity ≥ 80% of initial measured capacity.
The vacuum cleaner SHALL include a washable primary filter that can be removed and reinstalled without tools.
Filter removal and installation completed without tools in ≤ 60 s by a user following instructions; no damage after 10 wash/dry cycles.

versions.stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.

messages.content: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.

Stakeholders include end-users (home consumers), purchasers (may prioritize price/brand), manufacturer/engineering (feasibility, cost, supply chain), compliance/regulators (EU conformity), service/returns (reliability, maintainability), and retailers (packaging, claims). Current requirements specify three high-level constraints: minimum runtime (>=20 min), maximum weight (<1.5 kg), and compliance with EU safety standards. Key ambiguities affecting engineering trade-offs are: how runtime is measured (power mode, battery age, floor/usage pattern), what is included in the weight (battery, nozzle/accessories), and which specific EU directives/standards apply (e.g., LVD, EMC, RoHS, REACH, Battery Regulation, Machinery/Radio Equipment if applicable). Missing user-critical performance requirements (suction/pickup, dust capacity, charging time, noise, ergonomics, filtration/allergen needs) and lifecycle constraints (battery replaceability, durability, warranty) will drive design choices and verification criteria.
Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.

Stakeholders include: end users (ergonomics, suction performance on common home debris, noise, ease of emptying/cleaning, charging convenience), purchasers/households (price/value, durability, warranty), regulators/certifiers (EU compliance), manufacturer/service (assemblability, reliability, maintainability, battery lifecycle), and retailers/logistics (packaging, transport safety for Li-ion). Core requirements already stated: >=20 min runtime, <1.5 kg mass, EU safety standards; plus operating environment: indoor, 10–40°C, up to 80% RH. Likely additional requirement areas to formalize: cleaning performance metrics (suction/airflow/pickup on hard floors vs carpets, crevice tool performance), battery/charging (charge time, replaceability, cycle life, battery safety/transport), usability (one-handed operation, center of mass, handle design, bin volume, filtration/HEPA needs, noise limits), reliability (drop resistance, clogging tolerance, filter life), and compliance scope (applicable directives/standards: Low Voltage Directive if applicable, EMC Directive, RoHS, REACH, WEEE, Battery Regulation, IEC/EN 60335 series for household appliances, IEC 62133/UN 38.3 for batteries, and potentially Ecodesign/energy-related requirements).
